steps.title: Find the context for estimation
steps.content: The narrator writes “a safe guess would have placed him somewhere in the debatable ground between forty and fifty.” The phrase is fundamentally about positioning someone at a particular point on an age scale.

steps.title: Match the word to age estimation
steps.content:
The word "placed" needs to mean "estimated" — a guess positions someone at a point on an age scale between forty and fifty. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Estimated: "a safe guess would have estimated him somewhere between forty and fifty" — estimated means approximated by judgment, exactly what guessing someone's age does. ✓
  • Choice B — Contracted: "a safe guess would have contracted him between forty and fifty" — contracted means entered into an agreement or shrunk; neither sense applies to estimating age. ✗
  • Choice C — Adapted: "a safe guess would have adapted him between forty and fifty" — adapted means modified to fit a new purpose, unrelated to guessing age. ✗
  • Choice D — Arranged: "a safe guess would have arranged him between forty and fifty" — arranged means organized or planned, not judged or approximated. ✗
A is correct.
